spec: offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-001.md (US-270) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-006-env-example-ollama-base-url.md (US-271) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-040-release-version-seed-test-broken-by-sprint19-close-bump.md (US-272) + offices/pm/issues/I-015 + I-016 + I-017 records (US-273) + offices/pm/action-items.md AI-001 (US-274)
backlogItem: Sprint 23 is TD/issue/PM-tooling cleanup -- not a B-037/B-043 phase entry. Closes 3 long-lived TDs (TD-001 since 2026-01-29, TD-006 since 2026-02-05, TD-040 since 2026-04-30) + 3 stale issue records (resolved-in-fact months/sprints ago) + AI-001 phantom-path drift via sprint_lint.py extension.

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-22 close baseline per Rex US-266 verification (3833 PASS / 17 skipped pre-US-268+269; US-268+269 closed TD-042+TD-044 to bring to fully green ~3859). Verify exact count at sprint start. TD-040 closure (US-272) is itself a fast-suite hygiene win (one more red->green).

sprintNotes: GOAL: Cleanup sprint after Sprint 22 ship + V0.22.0 deploy. CIO morning testing tomorrow (Drain Test 7 + possible Drive 6) does NOT depend on Sprint 23 work; Ralph can run overnight independently. 5 stories, all TIER 1 (no deps), all small-to-medium scope. Total 4S + 1M = 6 size points. Comfortable overnight pace.

EXECUTION ORDER: All 5 stories are independent. Recommended: smallest first for momentum -- US-270 + US-271 + US-273 (records hygiene, S each), then US-272 (TD-040 shape-not-literal mirroring US-269's TD-044 pattern), then US-274 (sprint_lint.py file-existence check, the only M).

THEME: 'House cleaning while we wait for Drain Test 7.' Closes 3 long-open TDs that have been carried forward across sprints (TD-001 since Sprint 1, TD-006 since Sprint 6, TD-040 since Sprint 19), 3 issue records that got resolved-in-fact but never marked closed, and the phantom-path lint gap that Marcus has been carrying as AI-001 since Sprint 22 grooming.

CONSTRAINTS: standard Sprint Contract v1.0 + runtime-validation rule (US-272 + US-274 require synthetic tests that would FAIL pre-fix). Per CIO no-over-engineering directive (Sprint 22 close): keep changes minimal; do NOT add config plumbing or scope creep. US-272 follows the exact shape-not-literal pattern from US-269 (Sprint 22) -- one regex assertion swap.

GROUNDING SOURCES: TD-001 + TD-006 + TD-040 records (each contains explicit remediation recommendation with code samples) + I-015/I-016/I-017 issue records + MEMORY.md closure notes for each issue + offices/pm/action-items.md AI-001 + offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py current implementation.

POST-SPRINT-23 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json): (1) CIO + Spool run Drain Test 7 with Sprint 22 V0.22.0 deployed (logger CSV at /var/log/eclipse-obd/drain-forensics.csv once timer enabled). (2) CIO runs Drive Test 6 cold-start lifecycle. (3) PM bumps deploy/RELEASE_VERSION V0.22.0 -> V0.22.1 at sprint close (PATCH bump for hygiene-only sprint per SemVer rule). (4) Stale local sprint branch cleanup (9 now). (5) Optional CIO action: install rsync on Windows MINGW64 to speed up Pi deploy (one-time, ~5 min, drop-in replacement for tar fallback).

stories.title: TD-001 close: TestDataManager pytest collection warning
stories.intent: TD-001 has been open since Sprint 1 (2026-01-29). The `TestDataManager` class in `tests/test_utils.py` has a `Test` prefix, so pytest tries to collect it as a test class but can't instantiate due to constructor args, emitting a warning every test run. Two valid fixes per TD remediation: (a) rename to `DataManager` (disruptive -- breaks all imports), (b) add `__test__ = False` class attribute (one-line, no caller impact). Pick (b); least-disruption per CIO no-over-engineering directive.

filesToTouch: tests/test_utils.py (UPDATE -- add `__test__ = False` class attribute to TestDataManager class to suppress pytest collection)
offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-001.md (UPDATE -- mark Status: Resolved + closure date + commit hash)
doNotTouch: Production code in src/ (this is test-utility-only fix)
Any caller of TestDataManager (no rename, no API change)

groundingRefs.value: TD-001 open since 2026-01-29; pytest warning every run
Option (b) preferred: __test__ = False is one-line, no caller impact

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `TestDataManager` tests/ src/. Confirm scope is one class declaration in tests/test_utils.py.
tests/test_utils.py: TestDataManager has `__test__ = False`; pytest emits ZERO `cannot collect test class 'TestDataManager'` warnings on full fast-suite run
TD-001 record marked Resolved with closure date + commit hash
Fast suite passes; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
verification: pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' -q 2>&1 | grep -i 'TestDataManager' || echo 'no warnings'
invariants: TestDataManager API unchanged (no rename, no method signature change)
All callers of TestDataManager continue to work (compile + runtime)
stopConditions: If __test__ = False on a parent class also affects sibling classes used as actual tests -- STOP, document and propose rename instead

stories.title: TD-006 close: .env.example documents OLLAMA_BASE_URL
stories.intent: TD-006 has been open since Sprint 6 (2026-02-05). US-OLL-001 made `ollamaBaseUrl` configurable via `${OLLAMA_BASE_URL:http://localhost:11434}` placeholder syntax, but `.env.example` was never updated to document the new env var. Without doc in `.env.example`, fresh-checkout developers + Pi deployers don't know the override exists. Single-file mechanical addition matching the existing `.env.example` comment style.

stories.title: TD-040 close: shape-not-literal assert on test_releaseVersionFile
stories.intent: TD-040 P1 has been red on fast-suite since Sprint 19 close (2026-04-30 commit 5025508). `test_releaseVersionFile_seedVersionIsV0_18_0` asserts literal `'V0.18.0'` but PM bumps RELEASE_VERSION at every sprint close per `feedback_pm_sprint_close_version_bump.md`, so the test breaks every sprint and PM forgets to update it. Mirror US-269's TD-044 fix exactly: rename to `test_releaseVersionFile_holdsValidSemver` and assert `r'^V\d+\.\d+\.\d+$'` regex shape + non-empty description. Stable across all future bumps. Closes a 4-sprint hygiene wound.

filesToTouch: tests/deploy/test_release_versioning.py (UPDATE -- rename test method to test_releaseVersionFile_holdsValidSemver; assert SemVer-shape regex + non-empty description; preserve any other assertions in the test)

stories.title: AI-001 close: sprint_lint.py file-existence check on filesToTouch
stories.intent: 9-session pattern of phantom paths in `sprint.json` `scope.filesToTouch` (Sprints 14-22; latest example Sprint 22 US-264 listed `src/pi/display/dashboard_layout.py` but actual path is `src/pi/hardware/dashboard_layout.py`). Marcus's PM-side template-generator drift; Ralph wastes audit time tracking down the real path on every occurrence. Fix: extend `offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py` with a check that walks every story's `scope.filesToTouch`, parses out the path (strips parenthetical commentary), and verifies UPDATE paths exist in the current repo. NEW paths (annotated with `(NEW ...)` in the parenthetical) are exempt -- they're SUPPOSED to not exist yet.

filesToTouch: offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py (UPDATE -- add lintFilesToTouchPaths function: walks each story.scope.filesToTouch list, parses path-vs-commentary on first ` (` separator, classifies via parenthetical content (NEW = exempt, UPDATE/no-annotation = require exists), returns list of error strings for missing UPDATE paths; wired into lintStory())

tests/pm/test_sprint_lint_filestotouch.py (NEW -- test cases: NEW path missing-on-disk -> no error; UPDATE path missing-on-disk -> error; UPDATE path present-on-disk -> no error; no-annotation path missing -> error; path with sub-parenthetical commentary -> correctly stripped; would FAIL pre-fix because current sprint_lint.py has no such check)
filesToRead: offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py (current implementation -- understand SIZE_CAPS + REQUIRED_FIELDS + lintStory pattern + main loop)
offices/ralph/sprint.json (Sprint 23 contract -- use as test fixture for the new check; should pass since we audited paths during grooming)
doNotTouch: Existing lintStory checks (sizing caps, banned phrases, etc. -- additive only)
